description_long: This is a round, deep bowl with a wide opening and a gently curving profile, made from a dark, matte ceramic material. The surface shows signs of age and wear, with visible chips and abrasions along the rim and body, as well as a few lighter patches that may indicate repairs or surface damage. The interior is smooth but not glossy, matching the exterior in color and texture. The bowl has a slightly uneven rim, suggesting it may have been hand-formed. Its overall color is a dark, earthy brown with some lighter, weathered spots. There are no visible decorative patterns or ornamentation, giving it a utilitarian appearance.
description: A dark, round ceramic bowl with a wide opening, visible chips, and a matte, weathered surface.
description_view_0: The bowl is seen from a slightly elevated angle, showing a chipped rim and a lighter patch on the exterior.
description_view_1: This view highlights a prominent repair or patch on the side and additional rim chips.
description_view_2: The bowl appears more uniform, with fewer visible chips and a smoother rim.
description_view_3: The rim shows unevenness and wear, with the interior and exterior surfaces both visible.
attribute_location: kitchen or outdoor communal cooking area

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
